metadata.title: VesselNN Dataset Integration & Simulated Dataset Growth

task.title: Fix download_vesselnn() to clone the dataset repo
task.description:
The current downloaders.py has _VESSELNN_URL pointing to github.com/petteriTeikari/vesselNN (the CODE repo, ~67 MB). The actual TIFF data lives in github.com/petteriTeikari/vesselNN_dataset (~234 MB, separate repo). Fix the URL and update all registry entries.
Also fix source_format in acquisition_registry.py: VesselNN contains TIFF files (not NIfTI as currently declared). This affects whether convert_formats_task() runs for this dataset.
BACKWARD COMPATIBILITY NOTE: Changing source_format from "nifti" to "tiff" will change existing acquisition flow behavior — convert_formats_task() will no longer skip VesselNN. Tests that assert source_format=="nifti" must be updated. This is correct behavior (the registry was wrong).
NAMING NOTE: Two downloader files exist in the codebase: - downloaders.py (plural) — VesselNN + dispatch registry (edit this one) - downloader.py (singular) — MiniVess/EBRAINS download (do NOT edit)

tests.test: test_download_vesselnn_calls_git_clone — mock subprocess.run, verify correct URL used
test_download_vesselnn_skip_existing — returns immediately if .git dir exists

task.acceptance: URL points to vesselNN_dataset; source_format is "tiff"; acquisition_registry, external_datasets, and downloaders all agree on URL.

task.title: Create VesselNN filename pairing table
task.description:
In the vesselNN_dataset repo, filenames between denoised/ (images) and labels/ directories DO NOT match. Each of the 3 acquisition campaigns uses different naming conventions. A hardcoded pairing table is necessary because the mismatch is in the original source data.
The table maps each of the 12 denoised TIFF files to its corresponding label TIFF file, assigns a standardized name (vesselnn_01 through vesselnn_12), and records the campaign (Burgess2014, Poon2015, Santos2015).
Pairing (from vesselNN_dataset repo inspection): 1. burgess2014_bbbDisruption_BM4D_denoised.tif ↔ burgess2014_bbbDisruption_labels_v1.tif 2. burgess2014_lowerRes_hiSNR_BM4D_denoised.tif ↔ burgess2014_lowerRes_hiSNR_labels_v1.tif 3. burgess2014_noisySparseVessels_BM4D_denoised.tif ↔ burgess2014_noisySparseVessels_labels_v1.tif 4. burgess2014_tgMouse_BM4D_denoised.tif ↔ burgess2014_tgMouse_labels_v1.tif 5. poon2015_BBB_Leakage_BM4D_denoised.tif ↔ poon2015_BBB_Leakage_manualLabels_v2.tif 6. poon2015_BBB_noLeakage_BM4D_denoised.tif ↔ poon2015_BBB_noLeakage_manualLabel_v2.tif 7. poon2015_mixedSize1_BM4D_denoised.tif ↔ poon2015_mixedSize1_manualLabel_v2.tif 8. poon2015_mixedSize2_BM4D_denoised.tif ↔ poon2015_mixedSize2_labels_v1.tif 9. poon2015_plaqueVessels_BM4D_denoised.tif ↔ poon2015_plaqueVessels_label_underSegmented.tif 10. santos2015_lowContrastVessels_BM4D_denoised.tif ↔ santos2015_lowContrastVessels_labelsMarc.tif 11. santos2015_mixedSizelowContrastVessels_BM4D_denoised.tif ↔ santos2015_mixedSizelowContrastVessels_labelsMarc.tif 12. santos2015_tumor_BM4D_denoised.tif ↔ santos2015_tumor_initLabel_v1.tif
NOTE: These filenames must be verified against the actual repo. If any filename is wrong, the prepare step will fail with a clear FileNotFoundError listing the expected and actual files.

tests.test: test_pairing_table_has_required_keys — each entry has image_filename, label_filename, campaign, standardized_name
test_get_vesselnn_pairs_validates_existence — raises FileNotFoundError on missing file with helpful message
test_get_vesselnn_pairs_returns_tuples — returns list of (image_path, label_path, standardized_name)

task.acceptance: Table has 12 entries spanning 3 campaigns; standardized names sequential; manifest JSON validates; no duplicate filenames.

task.title: Create prepare_vesselnn_task() in acquisition flow
task.description:
After download_vesselnn() clones the dataset repo, the TIFF files need to be: (a) paired via T2's table (b) converted to NIfTI via existing convert_tiff_to_nifti() (c) placed in standardized images/ + labels/ directories
The prepare function: 1. Reads TIFF files from clone_dir/denoised/ and clone_dir/labels/ 2. Uses get_vesselnn_pairs() to match them 3. Converts each pair to NIfTI with voxel_spacing=(0.5, 0.5, 1.0) 4. Saves as output_dir/images/vesselnn_NN.nii.gz + output_dir/labels/vesselnn_NN.nii.gz 5. Writes pairing_manifest.json to output_dir/
Integration into acquisition_flow.py: - After download_dataset_task() returns DOWNLOADED for vesselnn - Call prepare_vesselnn_task() (which handles TIFF→NIfTI internally) - Skip convert_formats_task() for vesselnn (preparation already handles it)

tests.test: test_prepare_vesselnn_skip_existing — skip_existing=True returns early if images/ populated
test_acquisition_flow_calls_prepare_for_vesselnn — mock, verify task called after download

task.acceptance: Synthetic TIFF fixtures converted to 12 NIfTI pairs with correct names, spacing, and manifest; acquisition flow wires task correctly.

task.title: DVC versioning for VesselNN and composite datasets
task.description:
After VesselNN is prepared, create DVC tracking for: 1. The VesselNN dataset itself (data/raw/vesselnn → data/external/vesselnn.dvc) 2. A composite dataset (data/composite/v{date}/) combining MiniVess + VesselNN
The composite dataset is a directory containing symlinks (or copies) to NIfTI files from all constituent datasets, with a composite_manifest.json recording which datasets contributed, their DVC hashes, and volume counts.
Why physical directory (not just metadata): Docker containers need actual files on volume-mounted paths. Symlinks across mount points fail silently. MONAI CacheDataset needs a flat directory of NIfTI files.
DVC versioning scheme: - Individual: data/vesselnn/v{version} (git tags) - Composite: data/composite/v{date} (git tags) - composite_manifest.json records constituent dataset hashes

tests.test: test_composite_dataset_version_dataclass — fields: datasets, version, created_at, hashes
test_create_composite_version_writes_manifest — JSON with constituent dataset names + hashes
test_dvc_commit_handles_composite — mock subprocess, verify dvc add on composite dir

task.acceptance: Composite directory created with manifest; DVC add invoked; deterministic hashing for change detection.

task.title: Extract features from VesselNN volumes via data flow task
task.description:
New @task in data_flow.py that loads NIfTI volumes from an external dataset directory and extracts the 9 statistical features (mean, std, min, max, p5, p95, snr, contrast, entropy) using existing extract_batch_features().
The extracted features are returned as a DataFrame and optionally logged as an MLflow artifact (CSV) for downstream drift detection.
IMPLEMENTATION NOTE: extract_batch_features() takes list[NDArray[np.float32]], NOT file paths. This task must: 1. Discover NIfTI files in the dataset directory (glob *.nii.gz) 2. Load each volume via nibabel: nib.load(path).get_fdata().astype(np.float32) 3. Pass the list of arrays to extract_batch_features() This NIfTI loading step is the new code; feature extraction reuses existing code.
This task is generic — works for any external dataset, not just VesselNN.

tests.test: test_extracted_features_schema_matches_reference — same column names as MiniVess features
test_extract_features_logs_artifact — mock MLflow, verify CSV artifact logged

task.acceptance: 9-feature DataFrame extracted from NIfTI volumes; schema matches reference; empty directory handled gracefully.

task.title: Cross-dataset drift detection (MiniVess vs external)
task.description:
New @task in data_flow.py that runs FeatureDriftDetector for each external dataset against the MiniVess reference features. This is the "natural drift" use case — VesselNN data from different labs shows genuine covariate shift.
This serves as ground truth for drift detection sensitivity calibration: we EXPECT to see drift between datasets from different labs/microscopes, and the magnitude tells us how sensitive our detector is.
The task: 1. Takes reference_features (MiniVess) and a dict of external DataFrames 2. Runs FeatureDriftDetector for each external dataset 3. Calls persist_drift_reports() per external dataset inside mlflow.start_run() 4. Returns per-dataset DriftDetectionResult with per-feature breakdown 5. Logs results under drift_reports/{dataset_name}/ in MLflow
IMPLEMENTATION NOTE: persist_drift_reports() requires: - tmp_dir: Path — for intermediate HTML/JSON files (use volume-mounted path, NOT /tmp or tempfile.mkdtemp per CLAUDE.md Rule #18) - Must be called inside an active mlflow.start_run() context - Each external dataset gets its own MLflow run for artifact isolation

tests.test: test_detects_drift_on_shifted_features — synthetic features with shifted distributions
test_returns_per_dataset_results — dict keyed by dataset name
test_drift_reports_saved_per_dataset — artifact path includes dataset name

task.acceptance: Drift detected between shifted synthetic features; per-feature p-values available; MLflow artifacts saved under drift_reports/{dataset_name}/.

task.title: DatasetGrowthSimulator with 4 growth scenarios
task.description:
New library class implementing 4 dataset growth simulation scenarios for drift monitoring validation. Each scenario measures how drift scores change as the dataset composition changes.
Scenario A: Clean Addition - Merge VesselNN features into MiniVess features without modification - Measure drift score of combined vs reference (MiniVess-only) - Represents: "New lab joins study, data is genuine"
Scenario B: Drifted Addition - Apply synthetic drift (from drift_synthetic.py) to VesselNN features before merge - Parameterized by drift_type and severity - Represents: "New data has quality problems"
Scenario C: Incremental Growth - Add VesselNN volumes one-by-one (1 vol, 2 vols, ..., 12 vols) - Track drift score evolution curve - Represents: "How fast does drift compound with more external data?"
Scenario D: Temporal Simulation - Use SyntheticAcquisitionSimulator with real VesselNN volumes as base - Apply progressive drift schedules (linear, exponential, step) - Extract features from each drifted volume AFTER applying drift - Represents: "What happens over time as microscope degrades?"
IMPLEMENTATION NOTE: SyntheticAcquisitionSimulator operates on torch.Tensor volumes, not DataFrames. Scenario D must bridge the two domains: 1. Load VesselNN NIfTI as numpy → convert to torch.Tensor 2. Apply drift via SyntheticAcquisitionSimulator.generate_timepoint() 3. Convert drifted tensors back to numpy 4. Extract features via extract_volume_features() 5. Build DataFrame for FeatureDriftDetector Steps 1-5 are an adapter function, not changes to the simulator itself.
The class is a pure computation — no Prefect/Docker dependency. A @task-wrapped entry point in data_flow.py invokes it.

tests.test: test_growth_scenario_result_dataclass — fields: scenario_name, drift_score, feature_scores, n_volumes_total
test_scenario_drifted_addition_increases_score — severity=0.5 produces higher drift than severity=0.0
test_scenario_incremental_growth_returns_curve — list of GrowthScenarioResult with increasing n_volumes
test_incremental_curve_monotonic_for_drifted_data — drift score non-decreasing with more drifted volumes
test_scenario_temporal_uses_schedules — drift schedule severity affects output

task.acceptance: All 4 scenarios produce GrowthScenarioResult; incremental curve is a list; drifted addition increases drift score vs clean addition.

task.title: Model generalization assessment on VesselNN
task.description:
Load existing MiniVess-trained model checkpoints and evaluate on VesselNN WITHOUT retraining. This tells us: "How much does our model degrade when exposed to data from a different lab?"
The assessment: 1. Discovers most recent training run via FlowContract 2. Loads model checkpoint (best_val_loss.pth) 3. Builds MONAI DataLoader for VesselNN NIfTI volumes 4. Runs sliding_window_inference on each volume 5. Computes metrics: DSC, clDice per volume 6. Compares MiniVess-only metrics vs VesselNN metrics 7. Logs generalization gap: delta_dsc = mean(vesselnn_dsc) - mean(minivess_dsc) 8. Saves to MLflow experiment: minivess_generalization_assessment
This runs in the analysis flow container (GPU required for inference). The generalization config is a YAML experiment config, not hardcoded.
SCOPE NOTE: Adding external_test_dirs to run_analysis_flow() is a non-trivial signature change. The function currently has no such parameter. This change must also update: - PipelineTriggerChain in trigger.py (if it passes kwargs to analysis flow) - Docker Compose flow definitions (if params are env-var-driven) - Any deployment configurations that reference the flow signature The external_test_dirs parameter MUST have a default (None) to avoid breaking existing callers.
NOTE: This task requires GPU and real model weights. The TDD tests use mock models and synthetic NIfTI to test the pipeline logic, not the actual inference accuracy.

tests.test: test_evaluation_logs_per_dataset_metrics — mock model, verify eval_vesselnn_all_dsc logged
test_generalization_gap_computed — delta_dsc = vesselnn_dsc - minivess_dsc logged as metric

task.acceptance: Generalization constant registered; config parseable; analysis flow accepts external test dirs; per-dataset metrics and generalization gap logged.

task.title: Register VesselNN campaign subsets for per-campaign evaluation
task.description:
The 12 VesselNN volumes come from 3 acquisition campaigns. Registering them as DatasetSubsets enables per-campaign evaluation (Burgess 2014 performance vs Poon 2015 vs Santos 2015).
This reveals whether model degradation is uniform across campaigns or concentrated in specific acquisition conditions (e.g., low contrast, tumor vasculature, BBB disruption).
Subsets: - burgess_2014: volumes 1-4 (BBB disruption, sparse vessels, transgenic, low-res) - poon_2015: volumes 5-9 (BBB leakage, mixed-size, plaque vasculature) - santos_2015: volumes 10-12 (low contrast, mixed-size low contrast, tumor)

tests.test: test_subset_indices_cover_all_12 — union of all indices = {0..11}, no overlaps

task.acceptance: 3 campaign subsets with correct volume indices; full coverage of all 12 volumes.

task.title: Dashboard dataset growth and drift visualization panel
task.description:
New dashboard section showing dataset growth over time with associated drift scores. This panel helps researchers answer: - "How much has our data distribution changed since the last version?" - "Which features drift most when adding external data?" - "How does model performance change with dataset composition?"
The section includes: - Timeline of DVC versions with constituent datasets - Drift score per version (Tier 1 dataset_drift_score) - Feature-level drift breakdown (which of 9 features drift most) - Generalization gap per dataset version (if available) - Growth scenario results (clean/drifted/incremental curves)
Data source: MLflow artifacts from drift detection and generalization runs.
IMPLEMENTATION NOTE: DataDashboardSection is @dataclass(frozen=True). New fields (dvc_versions, drift_scores, etc.) MUST have defaults (None or empty dict) to avoid breaking existing instantiation sites in collect_data_section_task(). A separate DatasetGrowthDashboardSection dataclass is preferred — add it as a new section rather than modifying the frozen DataDashboardSection.

tests.test: test_dataset_growth_section_dataclass — fields: dvc_versions, drift_scores, feature_breakdown, generalization_gap
test_collect_growth_queries_mlflow — mock MLflow client, verify artifact queries
test_growth_section_handles_no_data — returns empty/status when no growth runs exist

task.acceptance: Dashboard includes growth section; markdown report has drift table; graceful handling when no data available.

task.title: Extend dashboard with cross-dataset drift comparison
task.description:
Extend the existing DataDashboardSection to include cross-dataset drift results — a table showing drift scores between MiniVess and each external dataset, with per-feature breakdown.
This supplements the existing drift_summary (temporal drift) with spatial drift (between datasets from different labs/instruments).

tests.test: test_cross_dataset_drift_handles_missing — None/empty when no external data

task.acceptance: Dashboard shows per-external-dataset drift scores; handles missing data gracefully.

task.title: Update docs/datasets/README.md with VesselNN preparation details
task.description: Expand the VesselNN section in the dataset README with: - Preparation instructions (automatic via acquisition flow) - Filename pairing note (why standardized names are needed) - Campaign descriptions with volume counts - Expected on-disk layout after preparation - "Composite Datasets" section documenting data/composite/ versioning
task.acceptance: README documents the full VesselNN pipeline and composite versioning.

task.title: Add configs/data/vesselnn.yaml for Hydra-zen composition
task.description:
Hydra-zen config file for the VesselNN dataset. Used by compose_experiment_config() when an experiment references vesselnn.
Contents: - data_dir: data/raw/vesselnn - n_volumes: 12 - split_mode: none (external test only — no training splits) - campaigns: list of {name, volumes} for each acquisition campaign - voxel_spacing: [0.5, 0.5, 1.0]
task.acceptance: Config parseable with correct metadata; split_mode=none for test-only use.

task.title: Integration test: VesselNN preparation to drift detection
task.description:
End-to-end integration test using synthetic TIFF fixtures (not real data). Exercises the complete pipeline: 1. Create synthetic TIFF files mimicking vesselNN_dataset layout 2. Run prepare_vesselnn() → produces NIfTI pairs 3. Extract features from prepared volumes 4. Run cross-dataset drift detection (synthetic ref vs synthetic vesselnn) 5. Create composite dataset version 6. Run incremental growth scenario 7. Verify all MLflow artifacts produced
Marked @pytest.mark.integration — excluded from staging tier.

tests.test: test_vesselnn_tiff_to_nifti_pipeline — synthetic TIFFs → NIfTI pairs with correct spacing

task.acceptance: Full pipeline exercises all phases with synthetic data; all artifacts produced.
